content_markdown: $4.0\text{ kg}$ of water at $100^{\circ}\text{C}$ is mixed with $1.0\text{ kg}$ of water at $0^{\circ}\text{C}$ in a container insulated from the surroundings. Which of the following is the final temperature of the water?
vibe_explanation: Since both substances are water, they have the same specific heat capacity $c$. We can use the principle of conservation of energy: heat lost by hot water = heat gained by cold water. Let $T$ be the final temperature. $m_1 c (100 - T) = m_2 c (T - 0)$. Substituting the masses: $4.0(100 - T) = 1.0(T)$. This simplifies to $400 - 4T = T$, which means $5T = 400$, so $T = 80^{\circ}\text{C}$.
